UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores core information and preferences for a registered user of the Demokratia Portugal application. Authentication is handled by an external system, so no sensitive credentials are stored here.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The primary email address of the user, used for communication and identification within the application.

displayName.description: A user-friendly name chosen by the user for display purposes within the application.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., 'pt' for Portuguese, 'en' for English).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile's information was updated.

SimulationModelDefinition.title: SimulationModelDefinition
SimulationModelDefinition.description: Defines the types of economic simulation models available in the Demokratia Portugal application, including their structural requirements for inputs and expected outputs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SimulationModelDefinition entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the simulation model (e.g., 'Fiscal Policy Impact Simulator').

description.description: A detailed explanation of what this simulation model analyzes, its scope, and underlying principles.

availableInputParametersSchema.description: A JSON string defining the schema or structure of input parameters required by this model, used to guide UI input forms.

outputMetricsDescription.description: A JSON string or descriptive text outlining the key economic metrics and data points typically generated as output by this model.

version.description: Version identifier for the simulation model, allowing for tracking of updates and changes over time.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this simulation model definition was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time this simulation model definition was updated.

UserSimulationRun.title: UserSimulationRun
UserSimulationRun.description: Represents a specific instance of a user executing an economic simulation, storing their chosen input parameters and the resulting outputs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSimulationRun entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this simulation run. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserSimulationRun)

simulationModelDefinitionId.description: Reference to the SimulationModelDefinition that was used for this particular simulation run. (Relationship: SimulationModelDefinition 1:N UserSimulationRun)

title.description: An optional, user-provided title for this specific simulation run, for easy identification and organization.

notes.description: Optional notes or observations entered by the user regarding this simulation run, its context, or specific findings.

inputVariables.description: A JSON string storing the specific hypothetical political variables and their values provided by the user for this simulation run.

simulationResults.description: A JSON string storing the comprehensive output data and analysis generated by the economic simulation for the given inputs, including various metrics, charts data, and textual analysis.

runTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating the exact date and time when this simulation run was executed.

SavedDataView.title: SavedDataView
SavedDataView.description: Stores user-defined configurations for exploring public economic and social data, allowing users to save and revisit specific data visualizations or filtered datasets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavedDataView entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created and saved this data view. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SavedDataView)

name.description: A user-defined name for this saved data view or dashboard configuration, for easy recall.

description.description: An optional description provided by the user about the content or purpose of this saved view.

viewConfiguration.description: A JSON string defining the complete configuration of the data view, including selected data sources, filters, visualization types, and display options required to recreate it.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this saved data view configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time this saved data view configuration was updated.

FactCheck.title: FactCheck
FactCheck.description: Represents a single fact-checking request made by a user, storing the original claim and the AI-generated analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FactCheck entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this fact-check.

claim.description: The original claim or statement submitted by the user for verification.

verdict.description: The final verdict of the fact-check analysis (e.g., 'Verdadeiro', 'Falso').

explanation.description: A detailed explanation from the AI supporting the verdict, including context and data.

sources.description: A JSON string representing an array of URL strings for the primary sources used.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the fact-check was performed.

DataSource.title: DataSource
DataSource.description: Represents an external data source, either an API or a website, that can be used by the AI for analysis and fact-checking.

name.description: A user-friendly name for the data source (e.g., 'INE - Instituto Nacional de Estatística').

url.description: The base URL for the API endpoint or the website address.

type.description: The type of the data source.

description.description: A brief description of the data source and the kind of data it provides.

requiresAuth.description: Whether this data source requires authentication.

authMethod.description: The authentication method used if required (e.g., 'API Key', 'Bearer Token').

credentials.description: The credential string (API key or token). This will be stored as plain text and should only be used for non-critical, public data APIs.

isSystemSource.description: Indicates if this is a default system source. System sources cannot be deleted by admins.

definition.description: Stores core information and preferences for a registered user. The document ID (`userId`) MUST match the Firebase Authentication UID of the user. Includes denormalized 'id' field matching the path wildcard for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Contains definitions of economic simulation models. This is a global, publicly readable collection, with write access typically restricted to administrators for structural segregation and clarity of security posture.

params.name: simulationModelDefinitionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific simulation model definition.

definition.description: Stores individual simulation scenarios created by a specific user. The 'userId' field is denormalized into each document and also present in the path for robust authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns this simulation scenario.

params.name: scenarioId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user's simulation scenario.

definition.description: Contains user-defined configurations for exploring public data. The 'userId' field is denormalized into each document and also present in the path for robust authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user who owns this saved data view.

params.name: savedDataViewId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user's saved data view.

definition.description: Stores individual fact-check results for a specific user. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

params.name: factCheckId
params.description: The unique identifier for the fact-check.

definition.description: A collection used for database access control (DBAC) to designate administrative users. The existence of a document at this path for a given UID signifies that the user has admin privileges. This uses 'Existence over Content' for role assignment.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user designated as an administrator.

definition.description: Stores configurations for external data sources. Readable by all authenticated users, but only writable by administrators to ensure integrity. This allows AI flows to dynamically consult available data sources.

params.name: dataSourceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a data source.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, and Structural Segregation to ensure secure, scalable, and debuggable security rules. 

1.  **Authorization Independence (Denormalization for Rules):**
    *   For user-specific data like `UserSimulationRun` and `SavedDataView`, the `userId` is explicitly stored within each document and is also part of the document path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/simulationRuns/{simulationRunId}`). This denormalization is critical. It allows security rules to check ownership using `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` AND `request.auth.uid == {userId}` (from the path wildcard) without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This prevents hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule debugging.
    *   For `UserProfile`, the document ID itself is the `userId` (i.e., `/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)`), which intrinsically links the document to its owner, providing atomic ownership checks.

2.  **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
    *   `UserProfile` documents are individual, private resources, exclusively owned by the authenticated user. Their access rules are simple: `request.auth.uid == userId`.
    *   `SimulationModelDefinition` documents are global and publicly readable. This collection is designed for homogeneous public read access, with write access restricted to administrators (managed via a separate `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection for DBAC). This clear segregation simplifies rules, as all documents within this collection share the same access requirements.
    *   `UserSimulationRun` and `SavedDataView` are segregated under `/users/{userId}/...` subcollections. All documents within a specific user's subcollection share the same ownership-based security posture, making rules straightforward: `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == {userId};`.

3.  **QAPs (Query-as-Permission):**
    *   For user-owned collections (`/users/{userId}/simulationRuns` and `/users/{userId}/savedDataViews`), the path structure itself enforces permissions for `list` operations. Users can only `list` documents within their own `/users/{userId}/` segment, inherently preventing them from querying other users' data. This eliminates the need for complex filter logic in rules, fulfilling the QAP principle robustly.
    *   For `SimulationModelDefinition`, since it's globally readable, `list` operations are open, as intended.

4.  **DBAC (Database Access Control):** Global roles (e.g., for managing `SimulationModelDefinition`s) would be implemented using an existence check on a dedicated collection like `/roles_admin/{uid}`, allowing for a simple, data-based access control.

Regarding the reported `FirebaseError: Firebase: Error (auth/operation-not-allowed)`, this indicates that the authentication providers specified in the `auth.providers` section ('google.com') may not be enabled in your Firebase project's Authentication settings in the Firebase Console. This is an environment configuration issue and not a data structure problem; you will need to enable these providers there to resolve the error.
